pages.raw_text:
Seth Low, former mayor of New
York city. participating In a joint
debate at Alton with Congressman
Harry F. Rainey Of Illinois, declared
to 5000 people that J. w. Van Cleve,
president OF the National Manufac-
turers' association. Is the greatest
enemy Of organized labor in the
country.


Low also alluded to the report,
which, he said, had gained country
wide circulation, that Van Cleave
and members Of his organization
practically had forced the national
republican convention at Chicago to
adopt the anti-injunction plank as a
part Of it's platform.


IF the Sherman antitrust law in
terferes with the rights of labor"
said Low. sit should be repealed"


Low also scored Bryan for at
tempting to- force the guaranty of
bank deposits upon the people be-
fore the plan had more than
reached an experimental stage in
Oklahoma.


"The guaranty plan should have a
thorough trial." he said. before the
country is asked to accept it."


Yet Low is working for Taft.
